The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA VS. ELIAS GEORGOPOULOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Deposition On S.F. Adult Probation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 2, 2011, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA'S Motion For Protective Order Re Deposition On S.F. Adult Probation IS Granted. Judicial estoppel is inapplicable due to the dissimilar situations of the parties. The information sought from the probation department goes well beyond what defendant may need to impeach plaintiff. Sanctions denied. Defendant¿s position, while rejected, did not lack substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: SCHINDLER ELEVATOR CORPORATION VS. 490 POST STREET INVESTORS, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Party-Affiliated Witnesses Of Deft To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspection Documents Described In Depositio Nnotice And For Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF SCHINDLER ELEVATOR'S Motion To Compel Depo Of Party-Affiliated Witnesses Of Deft To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspection Documents Described In Depositio notice And For Imposing Monetary Sanctions IS Granted. All four persons must be made available for deposition prior to 12/9/11. Defendant¿s conduct in not serving any objections to the notice and then sending an email the day before the depositions were noticed is the kind of gamesmanship that the discovery statutes and the appellate authority construing them have condemned. The fact that the deposition notice (apparently inaccurately) referred to the deponents as ¿members¿ of defendant and has the wrong first name of one of the deponents does not justify defendant¿s conduct, especially where, as here, there is no evidence disputing plaintiff¿s contention that all four deponents are managing agents or officers of defendant or otherwise sufficiently affiliated with defendant to be deemed party-affiliated witnesses. The failure to serve timely objections waives any objections to the taking of the depositions, but not to questions posed at the depositions. All documents sought in the deposition notice must be produced at the depositions, except those for which a privilege is asserted. As to the documents withheld on grounds of privilege, a privilege log is required, except for documents reflecting communications with Mr. Bernstein after he was retained to defend this lawsuit. Sanctions of $907.50 are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defendant and must be paid by December 31, 2011. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. WATSONVILLE COAST PRODUCE, INC., A FOR PROFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 2, 2011, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT FIRST HEALTH GROUP CORP.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Paragraphs 26 through 28 of the amended cross-complaint allege that the bills were provided to First Health on December 30 and the repricing needed to occur by December 31. Cross-complainant does not alleged or suggested that it can allege any ultimate facts that First Health had a duty to reprice within this short time frame. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. WATSONVILLE COAST PRODUCE, INC., A FOR PROFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 2, 2011, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. Cross-complainant adequately alleges a duty by the Regents to provide the billings earlier than December 30. Section 1300.71 does not bar the claim against the Regents. The cross-complaint does not show on its face that the fourth cause of action is time-barred. Judge Kahn discloses that the general counsel for the Regents is a good friend of his, but that relationship does not have an affect on Judge Kahn¿s ability to be fair and impartial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
